subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Waterborne Diseases
description: A fast breakdown of Waterborne Diseases for builders.

hook.text: Contaminated water kills more people annually than all forms of violence combined.

buildup.text: Cholera, typhoid, and dysentery spread through water contaminated with human waste.

discovery.text: Cholera bacteria produce a toxin that causes the gut to dump fluid—victims can die in hours.

twist.text: Simple oral rehydration—salt, sugar, and clean water—reduces cholera mortality from 50% to 1%.

climax.text: Water treatment and sanitation systems are the greatest public health achievements in history.

punchline.text: Clean water isn't a luxury. It's the intervention that saved billions.

quiz.question: What simple treatment dramatically reduces cholera mortality?
options: Oral rehydration with salt, sugar, and clean water
